description: {"blocks":[{"key":"blmpq","text":"This protocol describes the nucleic acids preparations for the Tara Oceans expedition and is part of Viral to metazoan marine plankton nucleotide sequences from the Tara Oceans expedition. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":63,"length":4},{"style":"italic","offset":165,"length":4}],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":101,"length":86}],"data":[]},{"key":"fcopd","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9hpk4","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7ga5m","text":"","type":"align-center","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"er1m8","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":1,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":[]},{"key":"vv4h","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"30smg","text":"Figure 1: Overview of -omics analysis strategy applied on Tara Oceans samples.","type":"align-left","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":8}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7aobs","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"BAA2B54EE67C4D9BB0AEA108C5E5C943","url":"https://www.protocols.io/view/viral-to-metazoan-marine-plankton-nucleotide-seque-qv6dw9e"}},"1":{"type":"imageblock","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"36BFB0A81E004ABCBDA747FF7C58FC3A","height":"595","is_video":false,"mime":null,"original_name":"Figure 1.jpg","placeholder":"https://content.protocols.io/zhnfw26.jpg","source":"https://content.protocols.io/zhnfw26.jpg","width":"573"}}}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"dsg2t","text":"The 'Nucleic acids preparations' section describes different nucleic acids extraction methods applied to obtain DNA and RNA from the different plankton groups sampled during Tara Oceans Expedition.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":174,"length":4}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"cf5nh","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fqlec","text":"1. DNA/RNA extractions from size fractions 0.8–5 μm (or 0.8–3 μm), 0.8–2000 μm, 5–20 μm (or 3–20 μm), 3–2000 μm, 20–180 μm and 180–2000 μm (Method ID: Euk_ DNA_RNA_ext)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":168}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"1e39s","text":"Plankton from these size fractions was collected on membrane filters and targeted unicellular eukaryotes (protists), usually \u003c200 μm, and metazoans, usually \u003e200 μm.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7gnb7","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2q2ki","text":"The protocol applied for nucleic acid extractions was based on simultaneous extraction of DNA and RNA by cryogenic grinding of cryopreserved membrane filters followed by nucleic acid extraction with NucleoSpin RNA kits (Macherey-Nagel, Düren, Germany) combined with DNA Elution buffer kit (Macherey-Nagel). This protocol was derived from optimization and validation experiments in the de Vargas laboratory at the Station Biologique de Roscoff (France). In particular, this preliminary work aimed principally to adapt the efficiency of the cell disruption and DNA/RNA extraction steps in order to efficiently capture nucleic acids from protists and metazoans collected from sea water filtering. Tests were conducted on a mock community composed of 26 monoclonal strains from the Roscoff Culture Collection (http://roscoff-culture-collection.org/) and natural filter samples collected in Roscoff (ASTAN, SOMLIT sampling). During these tests, the cell disruption step was optimized by applying a mechanical cryogrinding method to be sure that cells were efficiently disrupted, minimizing RNA and DNA degradation. Three cryogrinding protocols were tested using a 6,770 Freezer/Mill or 6,870 Freezer/Mill instrument (SPEX SamplePrep, Metuchen, NJ): 1 grinding cycle at 5 knocks per second for 1 min, 1 grinding cycle at 10 knocks per second for 1 min, and 2 grinding cycles at 10 knocks per second for 1 min. Best RNA and DNA quantities were obtained using 2 grinding cycles at 10 knocks per second for 1 min. Then, three simultaneous DNA /RNA extraction protocols were compared: Trizol method followed by RNeasy purification kit (Qiagen, Hilden, Germany), NucleoSpin RNA kit combined with DNA elution buffer set (Macherey-Nagel), and Nucleobond kit (Macherey-Nagel). Best quality results (DNA and RNA integrity conservation, ratios A260/280 and A260/230) were obtained with NucleoSpin RNA kit combined with DNA elution buffer set.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":806,"length":38}],"data":[]},{"key":"ds04o","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"67l5c","text":"After validation, the procedure described herein was applied in the Genoscope laboratory on Tara Oceans filters from the size fractions cited above.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9r0ct","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3cub6","text":"2. DNA/RNA extractions from size fractions 0.2–1.6 μm and 0.2–3 μm","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":66}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"mor5","text":"Two different protocols were applied to these size fractions that mainly targeted prokaryotes. Viruses and giant viruses (giruses) were also recovered in these fractions although dedicated filters (e.g.,\u003c0.22 μm) and specific extractions protocols (described in Sections 4 and 5) were allocated for their analysis.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bm81e","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"90fjf","text":"3. DNA and RNA backups","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":22}],"entityRanges":[{"key":1,"offset":0,"length":22}],"data":[]},{"key":"9ilhi","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4pj5l","text":"After nucleic acids extractions, two RNA aliquots and three DNA aliquots were prepared for each sample. One aliquot was used for the library preparation and sequencing process, the second one was stored as a backup. The third DNA aliquot was used for a backup after a whole genome amplification (WGA) process described in the DNA and RNA backup section.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"a7sk5","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7h8m","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7u8jl","text":"4. Viral particle concentration and DNA extractions from size fraction \u003c0.22 μm (Method ID: virus_DNA_ext)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":106}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"1mtn4","text":"This protocol describes a technique to recover viruses from natural waters using iron-based flocculation and large-pore-size filtration, followed by resuspension of virus-containing precipitates in a pH 6 buffer.","type":"align-left","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7fhrp","text":" ","type":"align-left","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ano6h","text":"Briefly, FeCl3 precipitation was used to concentrate viruses from 20–60 l of 0.22 μm filtered seawater, which were then resuspended in ascorbate buffer (0.125 M Tris-base, 0.1 M sodium EDTA dehydrate, 0.2 M magnesium chloride hexahydrate, 0.2 M ascorbate). This Fe-based virus flocculation, filtration and resuspension method (FFR) is efficient (\u003e90% recovery), reliable, inexpensive and adaptable to many aspects of marine viral ecology and genomics research. Data are also available from replicated metagenomes to help researchers’ decisions on the impact of linker amplification methods from low input DNA, viral purification strategies, and library preparation and sequencing platform choices. Following resuspension, recovered viruses were treated with DNase I to remove free DNA, followed by the addition of 0.1 M EDTA and 0.1 M EGTA to halt DNase activity, and further concentrated to\u003c1 ml using an Amicon 100 KDa filter (Sigma). DNA was extracted using the Wizard Prep DNA Purification system (Promega, Madison, WI). DNA concentration was assessed with PicoGreen (Thermo Fisher Scientific). ","type":"align-left","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sub","offset":13,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2h21v","text":" ","type":"align-left","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"49n4","text":"All detailed protocols are listed by name and are documented and available at https://www.protocols.io/groups/sullivan-lab.","type":"align-left","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":2,"offset":4,"length":18},{"key":3,"offset":78,"length":44}],"data":[]},{"key":"8fjj8","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"67on0","text":"5. Preparation of single cell amplified genomes (SAGs) (Method ID: SAGs_amplif)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":79}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"c7rpm","text":"Single amplified genomes (SAGs) were generated and their taxonomic assignments were obtained as in Martinez-Garcia et al. with the following modifications. Samples for heterotrophic (aplastidic) cells were stained using SYBR Green I. Samples for phototrophic (plasidic) cells were unstained. No attempt was made to identify mixotrophic cells. Several 384-well plates containing single cells of each type were prepared from each environmental sample. Backup plates were stored frozen at −80 °C. Single cell amplifications were validated by using an aliquot for PCR with eukaryotic universal 18S primers. SAGs with positive 18S sequence were sent to Genoscope for whole genome sequencing. Upon arrival, 2.5 μl were removed from each well and used to generate an amplified DNA backup by WGA. The reactions were performed as described for DNA extractions (DNA and RNA backups) except that debranching reactions were omitted and instead amplified DNA was purified by QIAamp DNA Mini kit (Qiagen).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":4,"offset":99,"length":22},{"key":5,"offset":852,"length":19}],"data":[]},{"key":"7k2m3","text":" steps: Please select nucleic acids extraction method. Accommodate each filter into a grinding vial with 1 ml RA1 lysis buffer (included in the NucleoSpin RNA Midi kit) and 1% β-mercaptoethanol. 1% β-mercaptoethanol Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #M6250 undetermined, RA1 lysis buffer NucleoSpin RNA Midi Macherey-Nagal Catalog #740962.20 1 , β-mercaptoethanol

Subject to the following grinding program using a 6,770 Freezer/Mill or 6,870 Freezer/Mill instrument : 2 min pre-cooling time first grinding cycle at 10 knocks per second for 1 min 1 min cooling time final grinding cycle at 10 knocks per second for 1 min. (6,770 Freezer/Mill or 6,870 Freezer/Mill instrumen, (SPEX SamplePrep))

Resuspend the cryogrinded powder in 2 ml RA1 lysis buffer with 1% β-mercaptoethanol, transfer to a large capacity NucleoSpin Filter from RNA Midi kit and centrifuge for 10 min at 1,500 g. undetermined, RA1 lysis buffer with 1% β-mercaptoethanol 00:10:00, Centrifugation NucleoSpin RNA Midi Macherey-Nagal Catalog #740962
Add 1 ml RA1 lysis buffer with 1% β-mercaptoethanol. undetermined, RA1 lysis buffer with 1% β-mercaptoethanol
Recentrifuge the filter for 3 min at 1,500 g. 00:03:00, Centrifugation
Transfer the eluate to a new tube and add 1 volume of ethanol 70%. Mix well. 70% Ethanol Contributed by users

Elute DNA by three successive elutions each with 100 μl DNA elution buffer and store in sterile microtubes at −20 °C. NucleoSpin RNA/DNA Buffer Set Macherey-Nagal Catalog #740944 Undetermined, Storage undetermined, DNA elution buffer

Quantify DNA by a dsDNA-specific fluorimetric quantitation method using Qubit 2.0 Fluorometer instrument with Qubit dsDNA BR (Broad range) and HS (High sensitivity) Assays. Qubit dsDNA HS (High sensitivity) Assays Thermo Fisher Scientific Catalog #Q32851 (Qubit 2.0 Fluorometer instrument , Q33226, with Qubit dsDNA BR (Broad range) and HS (High sensitivity) Assays)
Optional: check DNA quality in a sample subset by running 1 μl on 0.7% agarose gel for 60 min at 100 V. 01:00:00, Running 0.7% agarose gel

Immediately continue RNA purification on the previous NucleoSpin RNA Mini spin column by digesting residual genomic DNA. Prepare DNase reaction mixture by add 10 μL reconstituted rDNase to 90 μL Reaction Buffer for rDNase in a 1,5 ml tube. Mix by flicking the tube. Apply 95 μL DNase reaction mixture directly onto the center of the silica membrane of the column. Incubate at room temperature for 15 min. undetermined, rDNase undetermined, Reaction buffer for rDNase NucleoSpin RNA Macherey-Nagal Catalog #740955
Incubate column for 15 min at room temperature. 00:15:00, Incubation
Wash the column with RA2 and RA3 buffers according to the manufacturer's instructions. NucleoSpin RNA Macherey-Nagal Catalog #740955
Elute RNA in 60 μl RNase-free water and store in sterile microtubes at −80 °C. undetermined, RNase-free water Undetermined, Storage
Asses quantity of extracted RNA with RNA-specific fluorimetric quantitation on a Qubit 2.0 Fluorometer using Qubit RNA HS Assay. (Qubit 2.0 Fluorometer instrument, Q33226, with Qubit RNA HS Assays)

Assay quality of total RNA by capillary electrophoresis on a Bioanalyzer 2100 instrument (or equivalent), using the RNA 6,000 Pico LabChip kit. (Bioanalyzer 2100 instrument , G2939BA, with RNA 6,000 Pico LabChip kit)

In order to reduce as far as possible the risk of residual genomic DNA, apply a further DNase treatment. The previous RNA extraction procedure includes an in-column DNase treatment. Based on previous experience with this method, this step is sometimes only partially effective and does not always preclude the presence of trace DNA in final RNA samples. DNA removal in RNA samples is essential to prevent the incorporation of any genomic material in the RNA-Seq library and consequently the misinterpretation of RNA-Seq data analyses.

Process the RNA samples further as follows: treat a quantity of 5 μg, or less, total RNA aliquots with Turbo DNA-free kit, according to the manufacturer’s DNase treatment protocol. Set up the reaction by mixing total RNA with 5 µL 10X TURBO DNase Buffer and 1 µl DNAse in final 50 µl volume. Mix by gently flicking the tube. Do not vortex. undetermined, Total RNA aliquots Turbo DNA-free Kit Invitrogen - Thermo Fisher Catalog #AM1907
Incubate the reaction mixture for 30 min at 37 °C. (1/2) Undetermined, Incubation 00:30:00, Incubation
Elute RNA in 9–15 μl nuclease-free water by two elution steps in order to maximize recovery. undetermined, Nuclease-free water

Purify the reaction mixture with the RNA Clean and Concentrator-5 kit following the procedure described for retention of >17 nt RNA fragments. RNA Clean & Concentrator-5 Kit Zymo Research Catalog #R1015
Add 2 µL DNAse, mix by gently flicking the tube (no vortex) and incubate the reaction mixture for 30 min at 37 °C. (2/2) Undetermined, Incubation 00:30:00, Incubation

Quantify purified RNA with Qubit RNA HS Assay. The efficiency of DNase treatment should be assessed by PCR. When dealing with large number of samples, this validation step should be done on a subset of samples in order to check the efficiency of the treatment.

Two different protocols are applied to these size fractions that mainly target prokaryotes. Viruses and giant viruses (giruses) are also recovered in these fractions although dedicated filters (e.g.,<0.22 μm) and specific extractions protocols are allocated for their analysis. Cut the filters into small pieces and wash with pre-chilled PBS buffer in order to eliminate the RNA Later. PBS buffer Contributed by users To avoid loss of any cell during the washing process, pass the washing solution through a glass cup filtration system including a filter of 0.22 μm pore-size.

Place the pieces of the filter and the extra filter in a 50 ml falcon tube with a mixture of beads (1 ml of 0.5 mm glass beads and 2 ml of 0.1 mm Zirconia beads) and 10 ml buffer RLT+1% β-mercaptoethanol. 0.5 mm glass beads Contributed by users 0.1 mm Zirconia beads BioSpec Products Catalog #11079101z Buffer RLT 1% β-mercaptoethanol Contributed by users undetermined, 0.5 mm glass beads undetermined, 0.1 mm Zirconia beads undetermined, Buffer RLT+1% β-mercaptoethanol

Transfer the supernatant to a new 50 ml falcon tube and centrifuge again for 10 min at 4,500 g. 00:10:00, Centrifugation
Transfer the supernatant (about 10 ml) to a new 50 ml tube, add 1 volume 70% ethanol to the lysate and mix by inversion four times. 70% Ethanol Contributed by users

Divide the mixture in two and load each volume separately (by successive 700 μl aliquots) in two RNeasy mini columns and filter using a vacuum pump. In this way, a better yield is obtained rather than putting the entire volume in a single column.
Wash each column with 700 μl Buffer RW1 and twice with 500 μl Buffer RPE according to the manufacturer protocol. undetermined, Buffer RW1 undetermined, Buffer RPE

Elute RNA from the membrane with 70 μl RNase-free water. undetermined, RNase-free water The extraction protocol does not include a DNAse treatment. Perform a DNAse digestion, as described at the end of Euk_DNA_RNA_ext section prior to use for sequencing library preparation.
Quantify the RNA using both a Nanodrop ND-1000 spectrophotometer (NanoDrop Technologies Inc) and Qubit Fluorometer.
